chapter_title: Carpets and other textile floor coverings
scope_note: Note 1: 'carpets and other textile floor coverings' = floor coverings where textile materials serve as the EXPOSED SURFACE when in use. Also includes articles with characteristics of textile floor coverings but intended for OTHER purposes (e.g., carpet used as wall hanging — still Ch.57). Note 2: floor covering UNDERLAYS are EXCLUDED from Ch.57.

inclusions.description: Carpets and other textile floor coverings, knotted, whether or not made up
Carpets and other textile floor coverings, woven, not tufted or flocked, whether or not made up, including 'Kelem', 'Schumacks', 'Karamanie' and similar hand-woven rugs
Carpets and other textile floor coverings, tufted, whether or not made up
Carpets and other textile floor coverings, of felt, not tufted or flocked, whether or not made up
Other carpets and other textile floor coverings, whether or not made up (including coconut fibre/coir mats, braided mats, needle-punched carpets, flocked carpets)

exclusions.condition: Floor covering underlays (rubber, foam, felt, textile — used UNDER carpets)
exclusions.action: Excluded from Ch.57 by Note 2. Carpet underlay of foam rubber → Ch.40. Underlay of non-woven textile → Ch.56. Underlay of felt → Ch.56 (56.02). The underlay is not the exposed surface when in use

exclusions.condition: Tufted textile fabrics NOT for use as floor coverings (tufted towelling, tufted dress fabrics, tufted furnishing fabrics)
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 58.02 (terry/tufted textile fabrics for non-floor uses) — when tufted fabric does not serve as floor covering. Tufted bathrobes → Ch.61/62. Tufted bathroom rug → Ch.57. The 'floor covering' intended use is key

exclusions.condition: Textile floor coverings presented as art works or collectors' pieces
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 97 (works of art, collectors' pieces, antiques) — antique carpets >100 years or authentic collectors' carpets → 97.06. Modern carpets regardless of value → Ch.57

classification_rules.rule: Note 1 — 'Textile floor covering' definition: exposed surface test and dual-use articles
classification_rules.description: Note 1 defines the heading scope by FUNCTION: the textile material must serve as the EXPOSED surface when in use (face-up, walked upon). This covers: woven, knotted, tufted, needled, flocked carpets; mats of coir, sisal, jute; braided rugs; rush mats. ALSO covers articles with characteristics of textile floor coverings but INTENDED FOR OTHER PURPOSES (e.g., a woven textile rug intended as a wall decoration — still → Ch.57 if it has floor-covering character). The physical structure and weave/construction determines 'characteristic' not the stated end use.

classification_rules.rule: Note 2 — Underlays explicitly excluded from Ch.57
classification_rules.description: Floor covering UNDERLAYS (used beneath carpets — not the exposed surface) → NOT Ch.57. Classify by material: rubber/foam underlay → Ch.40; felt/non-woven textile underlay → Ch.56; woven textile underlay (jute carpet backing) → appropriate woven textile chapter. The distinction: IS it the exposed walking surface? → Ch.57. Is it under the carpet, not walked upon directly? → NOT Ch.57.

classification_rules.rule: Ch.57 heading matrix: construction method determines heading
classification_rules.description: Five headings classified purely by CONSTRUCTION METHOD: 57.01 = KNOTTED (hand-knotted Persian/Oriental rugs, machine-knotted; individual yarn tufts knotted to warp). 57.02 = WOVEN (flat-woven including kilim/kelim, Schumacks, Karamanie; loomed pile woven). 57.03 = TUFTED (pile yarn needled/tufted through backing fabric and secured). 57.04 = FELT (needle-punched or pressed felt — non-woven structure of fibres). 57.05 = ALL OTHER: includes coir/coconut fibre matting, sisal mats, braided, needle-punched (if more correctly described here), flocked, plaited, cord mats. Construction identification is paramount.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 57.01 (knotted) vs 57.02 (woven) vs 57.03 (tufted) — careful construction identification
classification_rules.description: The construction distinction is critical: KNOTTED (57.01) = individual yarns tied/knotted around warp threads at each row, creating pile (hand-knotted Oriental/Persian, Turkish/Ghiordes or Persian/Senneh knot). WOVEN (57.02) = woven on a loom where pile yarns are interlaced with warp/weft; include flat-woven kilims (no pile, just interwoven weft). TUFTED (57.03) = pile yarns punched through a pre-existing backing fabric by needles and secured with adhesive/secondary backing. A surface-level pile appearance requires identifying whether pile was knotted, woven or needled through backing.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: Ch.57 (textile floor coverings) vs Ch.46 (plaited floor mats) vs Ch.56 (tufted terry bath mats) vs Ch.39/40 (plastic/rubber floor mats)
classification_rules.description: Floor mats in multiple chapters: Ch.57 = TEXTILE floor coverings (textile material as exposed surface). Ch.46 = PLAITED/WOVEN floor mats of plaiting materials (rushes, rattans, bamboo strips, palm leaves — non-textile plant materials). Ch.39 = plastic floor mats (PVC/rubber-like plastic, no textile exposed surface). Ch.40 = rubber floor mats. Ch.56 = textile articles including bath mats of tufted terry that function as bathroom floor coverings (when clearly bath-mat for bathroom, may be 56.08 or 58.02). The exposed surface material determines the chapter.
